name: Google Lens
short_description: A visual-search platform that lets a phone camera identify objects, read and translate text, and surface information about whatever the user points it at.
long_description: Google Lens turns a phone's camera into a query: point it at a plant, a landmark, a receipt, or a foreign sign and it recognizes the content and returns search results, translations, or actions. Announced at Google I/O 2017 and shipped on the Pixel 2, it is now woven into Google Photos, the Google app, Chrome, and Android. As a daily-life physical AI system it matters because it lets people interrogate the physical world by looking at it, normalizing the idea that the camera is an everyday input device for situated, real-time machine perception.

images.caption: Google Lens recognizing and extracting text from a photograph on a phone (March 2023).
images.alt_text: A smartphone screenshot of Google Lens highlighting and recognizing printed text within a photograph, with selectable text overlaid on the image.

sources.title: Google Lens
sources.access_notes: Background on announcement at Google I/O 2017 and rollout history.

interactions.title: Read a foreign menu through the camera
interactions.description: Hold the phone over a sign or restaurant menu in another language and watch the translated words drop into place over the original, repositioning as you pan and tilt.

interactions.title: Copy text off paper straight to your laptop
interactions.description: Point Lens at a printed page, highlight a paragraph, and hit "copy to computer" to drop it onto the clipboard of a Chrome browser signed in on your desktop.

interactions.title: Price-check a product on the shelf
interactions.description: Snap a photo of an item and Lens surfaces its price across retailers, reviews, and where to buy it; you can add words like "brown" or "velvet" to steer toward a closer match.

interactions.title: Photograph a rash to start a search
interactions.description: Aim the camera at a mole, bump, or skin patch you can't describe in words and Lens returns visually similar images to inform a search; Google is explicit that it is not a diagnosis.

interactions.title: Grab codes and book quotes you'd otherwise retype
interactions.description: People reach for Lens to lift a delivery tracking number, a serial key, or a passage from a paper book into a notes app, replacing a surprising amount of manual typing.

interactions.title: Foragers point it at mushrooms — and get poisoned
interactions.description: Despite being built for visual search, not edibility calls, people use Lens to ID wild mushrooms; one case saw it rate a toxic Chlorophyllum molybdites as a "Choice edible," landing two people seriously ill.

interactions.title: Decipher a doctor's handwritten prescription
interactions.description: Photograph a scrawled prescription and have Lens detect and highlight the medication names, reading them back to you — grounded in the handwriting-recognition prototype Google has demoed but not shipped.

interactions.title: Catalog a bookshelf by panning across the spines
interactions.description: Sweep the camera along a shelf and let Lens read each title off the spines into a running list — built on the same real-world OCR and object recognition it already runs for text and product capture.
